name: Adrian Ceballos
headline: Motorsport Engineer
summary: Motorsport Engineer with experience in Aerodynamic development and a strong focus on Trackside support as a Data/Performance Engineer.
bio: Motorsport Engineer who enjoys tackling new  challenges with a hands-on approach. Experienced in Aerodynamics and Trackside Activities, supporting teams competing in IMSA, EUROCUP3 and in off-road events like the Baja 1000 race.
description: I am a curious engineer who is constantly exploring for new project ideas to implement. I enjoy working with challenging tasks and applying my skills on a wide variety of topics to develop innovative solutions. From CAD modelling to evaluation of physical phenomena with CFD simulations and data analysis, I can confidently manage and optimise processes of Motorsport projects.

In previous roles I led the engineering department of a plastic injection consultancy company, performed set-up changes for off-road cars, directed wind tunnel tests and optimised designs in Formula Student and the EUROCUP3 championship. Most recently, I produced the aero maps of an Audi RS3 LMS using iRacing data to propose a real testing methodology for an IMSA team.

My academic background consists of a BSc in Mechanical Engineering from ITESO University in Mexico and an MSc in Motorsport Engineering from Oxford Brookes University. Additionally, I am certified in Trackside Aerodynamics and Aerodynamic Design with CATIA3DX by the Motorsport Engineer Network. I am comfortable implementing Machine Learning methods after completing an online Python for Data Science certification.
